Archaeologists find the largest extraterrestrial iron object from the Bronze Age in China, impossible to manufacture with the technology of the time
Source: La Brújula Verde    Published: 2026-03-17 17:01:58

LBV Magazine English Edition History & Culture Chinese scientists have confirmed that a peculiar iron object unearthed in the famous sacrificial area of Sanxingdui has an extraterrestrial origin. It is the first and oldest artifact made from meteoritic iron found in the south of the country. The discovery, published in the journal Archaeological Research in Asia, presents the results of analyses carried out on a metal piece shaped like an axe or elongated tool, about 20 centimeters long, which was recovered from pit number 7 of the sacrificial area of Sanxingdui. This site, one of the most important in China, dates from between 2800 and 600 BCE, and its period of greatest splendor coincides with the Shang dynasty (1600–1046 BCE). The eight pits discovered in the area have yielded thousands of ritual objects, such as bronze masks, figurines, and bronze trees, which have completely transformed the understanding of Bronze Age culture in China. The object in question, labeled K7QW-TIE-1, appeared embedded vertically at the bottom of the east wall of the pit. Its condition was very fragile, so archaeologists decided to extract it together with the surrounding soil and transfer it to the laboratory of the Sanxingdui Museum for conservation. During the initial cleaning process, three small fragments detached, allowing the first analyses to be carried out without damaging the main piece. Initial examinations using portable X-ray fluorescence already yielded a surprising result: the piece was composed of more than 90% iron, but also contained 7.41% nickel, an element very uncommon in terrestrial iron obtained through smelting. This chemical composition, together with the antiquity of the archaeological context—well before the development of iron smelting technology in China—led researchers to suspect that they might be dealing with meteoritic iron. To confirm this, the team took a sample from fragment 2 and subjected it to metallographic analysis and scanning electron microscopy with X-ray spectroscopy (SEM-EDS). The results left no room for doubt. When observing the piece under an electron microscope, scientists saw a microstructure composed of equiaxed ferrite grains, that is, crystals of pure iron of uniform size and without deformation. This structure is typical of metallic meteorites, which cool very slowly in space over millions of years. But the most revealing aspect was the distribution of nickel. The chemical maps obtained by SEM-EDS showed that nickel—with an average concentration of around 20%—was distributed completely homogeneously throughout the sample. This is something that, according to the authors of the study, is extremely difficult to achieve using any smelting technique known during the Shang period. Nickel and iron diffuse at very different rates, and obtaining such a uniform, nickel-rich alloy would have required technologies that did not exist until millennia later. In the words of the study, this chemical homogeneity in terms of high-nickel iron would have been extremely difficult to achieve using any smelting technology known during the late Shang period, further supporting a meteoritic origin rather than cast iron for this artifact. Receive our news and articles in your email for free. You can also support us with a monthly subscription and receive exclusive content. Type your email… Sign up Moreover, the dark areas visible in the microphotographs after chemical etching turned out to be cracks produced by corrosion, not different metallic phases. Spot analysis of these areas revealed high levels of oxygen and a decrease in iron and nickel, confirming that they are oxidation products. The Sanxingdui find is not the first meteoritic iron object identified in China. To date, thirteen such pieces are known across the country, distributed across various regions and periods. The oldest is a knife found in the Narensu necropolis, in Xinjiang, dated to around 3000 BCE. However, most of the objects are concentrated in the north (Beijing, Henan, Hebei) and belong to the Shang dynasty. In the south, only two examples had been found: the Sanxingdui piece and another in the Yejiashan cemetery, in Hubei. The Sanxingdui specimen stands out for several reasons. It is, so far, the largest in all of China. As for its shape and decoration, the authors note that, although intense corrosion prevents determining whether it had ornaments, it shows no evidence of inlays, unlike other northern objects that do feature turquoise inlays.In this respect, it more closely resembles finds from Xinjiang, also outside the cultural sphere of the Central Plains. However, differences in shape and deposition context indicate distinct regional metallurgical traditions, with no direct connections. Another striking feature is its very high nickel content, around 20%, well above the average of other known objects, which is about 11% and can range between 0.03% and 29.3%. One of the questions archaeologists are asking is what this object was used for. In the Central Plains, meteoritic iron was usually employed as cutting edges inserted into bronze weapons or tools, forming bimetallic objects associated with high-status burials. In those cases, researchers believe that artisans cast bronze around the already existing iron blade, embedding it in molds. By contrast, objects from Xinjiang and Sanxingdui are monometallic: they are made exclusively of meteoritic iron. The context of pit 7 at Sanxingdui is clearly ritual, but the exact function of the object is unclear. However, the authors point to an intriguing detail: many of the bronzes from Sanxingdui, including masks and vessels, show cut marks made after casting, suggesting that very hard tools were used to modify them. The meteoritic iron axe, being harder than bronze, could have been one of those tools. That said, researchers warn that more evidence is needed to confirm this hypothesis. The importance of the discovery goes beyond the piece itself. As the authors write in their conclusions, the material analysis confirms the meteoritic origin of artifact K7QW-TIE-1. As the oldest meteoritic iron artifact from the Bronze Age found in southwestern China, it fills a critical gap in the region's metallurgical records and provides new insights into the early use of iron at both regional and global levels. The presence of meteoritic iron in Sanxingdui also highlights a distinctive metallurgical practice in southwestern China, in contrast with contemporary practices in the Central Plains. The study also leaves open questions. For example, it has not been possible to determine which specific group of meteorites the iron belonged to. This would require more detailed analyses, but the fragile condition of the piece makes further cleaning or extraction difficult. The authors note that in the future it will be necessary to work with high-resolution characterization techniques to refine the classification of the meteorite and clarify the possible functional and ritual roles of the object. For now, what is already certain is that, more than three thousand years ago, in a ceremonial center in southwestern China, someone placed in a pit an object made from a material that came from the sky, and that gesture has reached us as a fascinating testimony to the technical ability and symbolic thinking of Bronze Age societies. SOURCES Haichao Li, Zishu Yang, Yuniu Li, Jiahui Liu, Yu Lei, Honglin Ran, The earliest meteoritic iron artefact of the Chinese Bronze Age discovered at Sanxingdui, Southwest China. Archaeological Research in Asia, Volume 46, June 2026, 100692. doi.org/10.1016/j.ara.2026.100692 Receive our news and articles in your email for free. Has Steven Spielberg Ever Had His Own Alien Encounter? The Disclosure Day Director Says…
Source: SYFY    Published: 2026-03-17 15:11:42

From E.T. to Disclosure Day, Steven Spielberg is no stranger to the concept of alien life. The irony? He hasn't had one genuine UFO encounter in his entire life. You'd think that a man who has made four theatrical movies about contact with extra-terrestrial life, both benevolent and malevolent, would have had at least one close encounter in his lifetime. Sadly, that hasn't been the case for Steven Spielberg, who is preparing for the release of his fifth alien-centric movie, Disclosure Day, which stars Emily Blunt (A Quiet Place), Josh O'Connor (Wake Up Dead Man), Colin Firth (Kingsman: The Secret Service), Eve Hewson (Behind Her Eyes), and Colman Domingo (The Running Man). But if any non-human entities outside of Earth are interested in giving the director that experience ahead of the film's June 12 release, the storied Hollywood icon is more than ready to accept the invitation. “I made a movie called Close Encounters of the Third Kind—I haven't even had a close encounter or the first or second kind!” Spielberg said at a SXSW panel in Austin, Texas March 13 (via The Hollywood Reporter). “Why haven't I seen anything? Half of my friends have seen UFOs or UAPs. Where's the justice of that? If you're listening out there..." Based on an original story by Spielberg and written by the director's longtime collaborator David Koepp (Jurassic Park, ​​​​​War of the Worlds), Disclosure Day centers around the concept of humanity being made aware of the fact that it is not alone in the universe. “I don't know any more than any of you do, but I have a very strong suspicion that we are not alone here on Earth right now—and I made a movie about that,” the filmmaker said. "Because no one should ever think that we are the only intelligent civilization in the entire universe. So I've been thinking as a kid that we were not alone ... The big question is: Are we alone now? And have we been alone over the last 80 years? Have we been alone over the last few thousand years?” As much as the government might want to cover things up (à la the infamous Roswell incident of 1947), people have the right to know the truth—even if that truth obliterates every preconceived notion they've ever held about their own existence. “I'm not afraid of any aliens," Spielberg continued. "I have no fears about that whatsoever. I think our movie does take into consideration that social dislocation that could occur. If it was announced there is interaction [with aliens] that have been going on for decades, it's going to cause a disruption in a lot of belief systems. But I don't think it is a lethal disruption at all." Disclosure Day is in theaters June 12. Stay up to date on your favorite shows and read all the latest news. Check your inbox for the latest news about your favorite shows and movies.

“Pants on fire moment:” missing US general with UFO links sparks online frenzy
Source: Cybernews    Published: 2026-03-17 13:30:00

© 2026 Cybernews – Latest Cybersecurity and Tech News, Research & Analysis. William Neil McCasland, a missing US general, is back in focus after Eric Burlison discussed his UFO/UAP links on the Weaponized podcast. The congressman revealed that whistleblower David Grusch had tried to reach him, deepening intrigue around the case. Former US Air Force Major General McCasland, 68, has been missing since February 27th after last being seen in Bernalillo County, New Mexico. McCasland's name appeared in a 2016 email exchange between musician Tom DeLonge and politician John Podesta discussing UFO-related topics. Though he has not been active in UAP programs recently, the historical connection has resurfaced in light of his disappearance. As Burlison and podcast host George Knapp pondered the general's disappearance, Burlison said McCasland had been “pretty difficult to communicate with.” Burlison added: “Every time someone has reached out, he has said that he has nothing to say.” He also claimed that Grusch, his senior advisor and a UFO whistleblower, had attempted to contact McCasland, but the general remained tight-lipped, fueling speculation that he may know more about secret government UFO programs. Knapp described the situation as “a pants on fire moment,” suggesting that something about the circumstances does not add up. The clip fueled speculation on the Reddit UFO community with users debating whether it implies dishonesty, secrecy, or something more unusual. Some Redditors view the situation as evidence of secrecy, with ThriceAlmighty writing: “Every bombshell… keeps it close to the vest and never shares anything.” Others are dismissive, with owl440 stating: “More conspiracy theory BS… they can't talk about it, so just keep tuning in.” A more cautious tone came from Grey_matter6969, who said: “I am hoping the general is safe and under protection… perhaps there are big wheels turning.” Illiminalll interpreted Burlison's wording as revealing, saying it “seems like an admission” tied to UAP knowledge. That view was challenged by Dinoborb, who responded: “That feels more like an assumption than an admission.” With such a fragmented online audience, split between suspicion, skepticism, and uncertainty,the intrigue deepens regarding McCasland's disappearance. There is currently no confirmed evidence linking McCasland's disappearance to UFO programs or foul play, and authorities continue to treat the case as an active missing person investigation. Nvidia's highly anticipated flagship artificial intelligence (AI) conference is on the horizon. But what will the company's CEO, Jensen Huang, reveal this year? First look: Gugu Mbatha-Raw stars in Sky Original supernatural thriller Possession
Source: Sky Group    Published: 2026-03-17 11:35:45

Sky has unveiled a first look image from Possession, confirming the new title for the Sky Original thriller formerly announced as Inheritance. Created and written by Karla Crome (Something Very Bad is Going to Happen) and directed by Storm Saulter (Better Mus' Come, Sprinter), Possession is a five part limited event series set between Bristol in the UK and Jamaica, led by Gugu Mbatha-Raw (Loki, Surface), Jonny Lee Miller (The Crown, Elementary), Sheldon Shepherd (Bob Marley: One Love, Better Mus' Come) and Bel Powley (A Small Light, The Morning Show). Claudia (Gugu Mbatha-Raw), an ambitious young bi‑racial lawyer, travels to Jamaica to challenge an inheritance claim made by a local man, Cudjoe East (Sheldon Shepherd), against Oliver Connaught (Jonny Lee Miller), a wealthy white aristocrat. But the case takes an unsettling turn when Claudia discovers that the disputed estate is the very place that has haunted her nightmares: Hope Hill, the Connaught family's former plantation. As Claudia and Oliver dig deeper, they uncover a disturbing link between the plantation's brutal past and the injustices still shaping the present. Possession is a bold, genre‑bending series that confronts the enduring legacy of colonialism, blending eerie mystery with the vibrant, restless energy of the Caribbean Alongside Gugu Mbatha-Raw, Jonny Lee Miller, and Sheldon Shepard, the series also stars Bel Powley as Charlotte, Nadean Rawlins (Bob Marley: One Love, Sprinter) as Mercy, Sean Gilder (Mary & George, Slow Horses) as Tom McKenzie, Olunike Adeliyi (The Fire Inside, Mike) as Patsy Dawkins, , Richard Dillane (Wolf Hall, The Crown) as Eugene Desfrienes, Diveen Henry (The Day of the Jackal, Manhunt) as Flora, Jack Bandeira (Happy Valley, Black Mirror) as Kenneth Connaught, Shantol Jackson (Death in Paradise, Yardie) as Sarah, Jeff Crossley (Bob Marley: One Love, Sprinter) as Angelo and Safina Simpson as Betina. Possession is produced by Snowed-In in association with Sky Studios. Written and created by Karla Crome, the series is based on an original story created by Jefferson Bannis and directed by Storm Saulter. Executive Producers are Ruth Kenley-Letts, Jenny van der Lande, Neil Blair, Karla Crome, Storm Saulter, Sam Hoyle, Serena Thompson, Gugu Mbatha-Raw and Jonny Lee Miller. The Development Producer is Roxanne Harvey and the Series Producer is Kate Ogborn. The series will be available on Sky in the UK and Ireland. NBCUniversal Global TV Distribution is handling international sales of the series. Contact: jessie.elliott@sky.uk, kwabena.agyare@sky.uk Possession forms part of a bold line-up of Sky Originals and Exclusives coming to Sky and NOW screens in 2026 and beyond. From gripping dramas and films to big entertainment and compelling documentaries, Sky continues to champion distinctive storytelling alongside world-class talent - delivering the must-watch moments set to define the year ahead. Possession will launch on Sky and NOW in 2026.

Former Air Force missile officer claims UFOs disabled nuclear arsenal at Montana base during Cold War
Source: New York Post    Published: 2026-03-17 08:57:00

Switch between CA and NY editions here. A retired US Air Force launch officer claims UFOs disabled multiple nuclear missiles when they appeared around the Montana base where he was stationed decades ago. Robert Salas claims a mysterious force paralyzed the military's ability to control 10 Minuteman I intercontinental ballistic missiles equipped with nuclear warheads at Malmstrom Air Force Base in 1967, the 85-year-old veteran said on the “Danny Jones Podcast” Saturday. Salas — whose job was to launch and monitor nuclear missiles “if given the order” during the Cold War — was at the controls underground with his partner around 10 p.m. March 24 of that year when panicked guards called from above about another unidentified craft. “The main guard calls down, says, ‘Sir, we've been seeing some strange lights in the sky, flying directly overhead,'” Salas said. Salas said guards insisted the craft weren't Soviet airplanes, pointing to bizarre lights as they zipped through the sky before suddenly stopping over the underground ICBMs. He initially brushed off the report, thinking it was a prank — but five minutes later, the guard called back, “screaming into the phone.” “He's yelling. He's babbling. He's frightened,” Salas recalled. After calming down, the guard told Salas his men had their weapons pointed at the craft hovering above the base's front gate, emitting a “pulsating reddish light.” Salas said the panicked guard asked what to do next, and he told him, “Do whatever you have to do.” The guard then said one of his men had just “got injured” before hanging up. Salas said he then went to wake his partner, who was resting, when “all of a sudden” a loud horn sounded at the controls, signaling that something was wrong with the missiles. “We looked at the board, and sure enough, one of them went from green to red. No ability to launch,” Salas said. “Then, very quickly thereafter, bing bing bing bing, all 10 of them went down. They all went red.” They jumped up and ran through their checklist, then realized two launch facilities miles away were showing that “someone or something” may have breached the fenced missile area. Salas said he immediately sent guards out to the missile silos, and when they were about a mile away, they saw the lights hovering above the launch areas. “They were scared to death,” he said. “They didn't want to go any further. They were so frightened of these things.” Salas said he later heard the injured guard might have hurt his hand while clearing a jam in his rifle or cut it on barbed wire during the encounter. He also learned guards claimed to have seen the craft in the area in the days before the encounter, craft that could reverse direction, snap into sharp 90-degree turns and fly in total silence. The former nuclear missile officer said a Boeing-led investigation could not determine what shut down the warheads, adding that the systems were designed to prevent jamming. “They had no idea how this signal could have been injected into each of the missiles,” he said. “The cabling system that we had was triply shielded against electromagnetic interference from the outside.” Salas said he's convinced that intelligent non-human civilizations visited Earth to prevent a nuclear war. “It's another civilization out there that is visiting us and are concerned about us destroying this planet through nuclear war, for many reasons, probably some we don't even understand,” he said. Air Force investigators forced Salas and his commander to sign strict gag orders following the encounter, warning they could face prison time if they ever spoke about it. He ultimately went public decades later after reading about a similar incident in a UFO book and concluding the information had already been exposed.

'New Nostradamus' who predicted Iran conflict makes chilling Donald Trump warning
Source: The Mirror    Published: 2026-03-17 05:22:06

The psychic has a chilling prediction about the President of the United States(Image: Yuri Gripas - Pool via CNP/Shutterstock) A famous psychic who calls himself the ‘Prophet of Doom' has made a surprising prediction about Donald Trump and his time in Office — and some people may not like it one bit. Craig Hamilton-Parker has been dubbed the ‘New Nostradamus' after claims that he correctly predicted major global events such as the Queen's death, Brexit, Donald Trump's 2016 election win and the onset of the Covid-19 pandemic. In more recent times, Craig claims he has correctly predicted the intricacies of the Iran conflict. In his prophecies, the soothsayer has long maintained that Iran would be at the receiving end of direct attacks on its military and nuclear infrastructure — a prediction that now seems to have come to pass. The British author and content creator is the host of a popular YouTube channel named after himself, where he uploads detailed videos of his predictions. Woolly mammoths will be back in 2028, how you could live to 200, alien signals lost in the weather, could we fight Godzilla, and a creature with 20 arms has been found near the Antarctic - all this and more in our latest weird science newsletter He calls himself the 'Prophet of Doom'(Image: Craig Hamilton-Parker/YouTube) His channel, Craig Hamilton-Parker, currently has 235,000 subscribers and each month, the renowned seer “unveils bold forecasts for our planet's future, drawing on ancient methods like India's mysterious Naadi leaves”, as per his channel description. Following the dramatic escalation of tensions in the Middle East, Craig's predictions are being re-examined and one stands out — the psychic foresees a third term in Office for Donald Trump. His prediction directly conflicts the 22nd Amendment to the United States constitution which states: “No person shall be elected to the office of the President more than twice, and no person who has held the office of President, or acted as President, for more than two years of a term to which some other person was elected President shall be elected to the office of the President more than once.” With Trump currently serving his second term, it would be impossible for him to have a third term on the horizon according to the Constitution, although he has publicly said in the past “there are methods which you could do it”. Backing up his third-term prediction, psychic Craig said: “To reiterate what I said at the time, I felt there would be some big global conflict, possibly involving Taiwan. Could President Donald Trump end up serving a third term?(Image: AP) “Looking at it now, it could be any global conflict. I know a lot of people have criticised this, saying you cannot have a third term because it is written in the Constitution-but who knows? Things have changed so much in the world.” In his prediction's defence, the soothsayer pointed at the shifting geopolitical order, offering it as proof that previously unimaginable scenarios have the potential to materialise in the blink of an eye. The famous psychic said: “Who would have imagined the possibility of invading Greenland, or kidnapping a country's leader, as has happened in Venezuela? The world is changing rapidly.” Predicting the use of emergency powers is a real possibility in 2026, the ‘Prophet of Doom' forecasted: “Something will occur that overturns the existing rules, and that period will be a time of great conflict.” For his predictions, Craig collaborates with his wife Jane and studies spiritual readings, including the ancient Indian practice of Nadi Astrology. His predictions cover a wide range of topics — from natural disasters and geopolitics to economic shocks and notable public figures. The psychic has repeatedly warned that 2026 will be a year laden with overlapping geopolitical crises — something we are seeing being played out in real-time. Supernatural Horror ‘The Other Side' Heads to Hong Kong FilMart With Contents Panda (EXCLUSIVE)
Source: Variety    Published: 2026-03-17 02:05:00

By Naman Ramachandran Contents Panda is bowing supernatural horror “The Other Side” at Hong Kong FilMart. Directed by Kim Jung-min and produced by Nalgae Entertainment (“Rhapsody for the Dead,” “Fantasy of the Girls”), the film stars Cha Sun-woo (“The Cursed,” “Reply 1994”) and Lee Lu-da (“Rinza Noodle House”), and is targeting a 2026 bow. In the film, three strangers — a writer, a creditor and a detective — converge on a remote cabin, each with their own agenda. Strange occurrences quickly destabilize the group, with the writer becoming increasingly unhinged, convinced the location has an inexplicable hold over her creative faculties. When a corpse turns up and evidence of a malevolent presence emerges, the detective sets about piecing together what the cabin is hiding while the others desperately seek a way out. Related Stories Have I Got News for You: CNN Keeps Digging Deeper Into Comedy CNN Staffers Fear Warner Bros.-Paramount Deal Will Hurt Their Journalism and Network's Financial Health The title joins two previously announced Contents Panda productions at the market. “The Second Child,” a mystery-dark fantasy-horror directed by Yu Eun-jeong (“Ghost Walk”) and produced by Film Run (“Moonlit Winter,” “Merry Christmas Mr. Mo”) stars Lim Soo-jung (“Cobweb,” “Woochi”), Park Soi (“The Atypical Family,” “Revenant”) and Yuna (“Dark Nuns,” “Pachinko”). The story follows a young woman who regains consciousness after three years in a coma, only to be confronted by the apparition of her deceased sister, as their mother's search for the truth behind the sister's death gradually uncovers a dark hereditary secret stretching back centuries. Their separate investigations converge when they encounter a stranger bearing an uncanny resemblance to the dead girl. Popular on Variety Also previously announced is “The Ultimate Duo,” a crime-action drama helmed by Park Chul-hwan (“Blood Free,” “Grid”), starring Bae Seong-woo (“Road to Boston,” “Beasts Clawing at Straws”), Jung Ga-ram (“Love Alarm,” “Jo Pil Ho: The Dawning Rage”) and Esom (“Kill Boksoon,” “Escape”), and produced by Generation Blue Film (“Jo Pil Ho: The Dawning Rage,” “Detective K: Secret of the Living Dead”). The film pairs a disgraced regional detective with an ambitious rookie, who built his reputation as a social media influencer before joining the force. The unlikely partners find themselves drawn into a Gangnam murder case that has already been officially resolved with a different suspect, forcing them to travel to Seoul to pursue what they believe is the real killer. It is targeting a release in the second quarter of 2026. A Variety and iHeartRadio Podcast The Business of Entertainment

Paranormal Cirque returns to the Valley with a thrilling, Voodoo-inspired show
Source: Hanford Sentinel    Published: 2026-03-17 00:00:00

Please log in, or sign up for a new account and purchase a subscription to continue reading. Please purchase a subscription to continue reading. Your current subscription does not provide access to this content. Editor/Lifestyles Paranormal Cirque is returning to the Valley and bringing big thrills, scares and laughs along with it — and maybe even a curse or two. “Voodoo,” the cirque's new show, will fill the Big Fresno Fairgrounds March 20-23. “There are impressive performances with creepy characters and horror-style effects. I would say it's more exciting than scary. It's intense and definitely different from a normal circus,” said acrobat and cast member Yuri Belaus. Different from a normal circus is an understatement. Paranormal Cirque is more in line with a visit to Hollywood Horror Nights or a burlesque show than anything Barnum & Bailey had in mind. Audience members have to travel through something of a haunted maze before even reaching their seats. “We are ‘rated R' because of some adult jokes, but there is nothing too scary. Just come to it, spend time with your friends. I always say, don't bring your kids — bring your friends,” Belaus said. Cirque Italia, Paranormal Cirque's parent company, describes the show as “a crazy yet fun fusion between circus, theatre, and cabaret in perfect harmony.” Throughout the show, the audience is taken on a narrative ride through a Voodoo-themed, haunted house-inspired cirque show that features death-defying acrobats, alluring magicians and spooky zombies. Belaus' acrobatic group is made up of four such daredevils who bounce around on horizontal bars “like monkeys,” he said. “Our show is more about stunts than anything else,” Belaus said. The troupe performing “Voodoo” is the cirque's original company, having formed in 2018. Paranormal Cirque currently has four traveling shows crisscrossing America at any one time. Hanford thrill-seekers may have caught the shows at the Kings Fairgrounds in 2022. That show was performed by the second company, which Belaus said is a more comedy-centric show and those who attend “Voodoo” will see a an all-new show that focuses more on thrills and danger than laughs. "But we do have a clown and he's hilarious,” Belaus added. Paranormal Cirque: Voodoo will run from March 20-23 at the Big Fresno Fairgrounds, located at 1121 S. Chance Ave. Tickets run $20-65 and are available at https://black.cirqueitalia.com. Parker Bowman is a lifestyles reporter and editor for the Hanford Sentinel. He can be reached by email at PBowman@HanfordSentinel.com, or by calling his office phone at 559-583-2432.
